Node Starter Kit: Programming with Cardboard

Discover More
The Core Shift

From Assembly to Iteration

Traditional kits teach you to follow instructions. NODE turns cardboard into an intelligent medium where you Design, Test, Fail, and Iterate. This is the missing layer in engineering education.

Why cardboard? Discover more

Our Solutions

For Education

Inspiring the future. Sparking curiosity. Teaching design and building skills for tomorrow.

Learn More

For Makers

A global community driven by innovation. Passion for hands-on iteration and creation.

Learn More

Check Out Some Reviews

Educator

"NODE has completely transformed how my students approach engineering. The iteration cycle is so fast!"

- Sarah J., Middle School Teacher
Maker

"I love how I can prototype with cardboard before committing to 3D printing. It saves so much time."

- Alex M., Prototyper
Professional

"The modularity of the components means I can reuse them across multiple projects effortlessly."

- David L., Hardware Engineer
Educator

"It's incredible to see the kids building physical systems so quickly. Highly recommended."

- Emily R., STEM Coordinator
Maker

"The perfect bridge between digital coding and physical crafting."

- Chris T., Hobbyist
Professional

"I use it for rapid sketching of physical interactions. It's an invaluable tool."

- Sam K., UX Designer
Educator

"Affordable, accessible, and endlessly creative. A must-have for any classroom."

- Jessica W., High School Teacher

Meet the Creators

Darren Chao

Darren Chao is a design engineer at Brown University and RISD and a UC Berkeley EECS alum. He creates thoughtful, human-centered objects and experiences, ranging from solving real problems to exploring playful and unconventional ideas. His work aims to make everyday life feel more intentional and alive. More of his work can be found at darrenchao.com.

Jialong Lai

Jialong Lai is a design engineer at Brown University and RISD, with a background in Industrial Design from Zhejiang University of Technology. He previously worked on boat manufacturing at Yaoyounguang Tech Group and now focuses on creating solutions that address real-world pain points. His work aims to make the world more accessible, and he has received recognition including Red Dot and iF Design Awards. More of his work can be found at jialonglai.cv.

Ruijia Diao

Ruijia Diao is a designer at Brown University and RISD, with a background in illustration from Parsons School of Design. She creates whimsical work that explores complex, intangible ideas across mediums including illustration, exhibitions, packaging, and projection mapping.
